compiled_state.title: rebar.__version__ says 0.3.0; installed package is 0.4.0
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Summary
rebar.__version__ reports 0.3.0 while the installed package metadata is 0.4.0.

## Reproduction Steps
(verified on installed 0.4.0 == HEAD)
1. python -c "import rebar, importlib.metadata as m; print(rebar.__version__, m.version('nava-rebar'))"
2. Output: 0.3.0 0.4.0

## Expected vs Actual
- Expected: module attr matches package version.
- Actual: src/rebar/__init__.py:22 hardcodes 0.3.0; pyproject.toml:10 is 0.4.0 (Release 0.4.0 commit 2a06570 did not bump the attr).

## Fix direction
Derive __version__ from importlib.metadata.version("nava-rebar") with a dev-checkout fallback; add a release-check test asserting module attr == package metadata so this cannot recur.

## Suggested RED test
test asserting rebar.__version__ == importlib.metadata.version("nava-rebar") — RED today, GREEN after.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] __version__ derived from package metadata (single source of truth: pyproject)
- [ ] Guard test prevents recurrence
- [ ] Works in editable installs (pip install -e) without crashing

verify_commands.dd_text: module attr matches package metadata (guard test)
